# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Pull Request Build

on:
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v2

    - name: Set up Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@v2
      with:
        python-version: '3.8'

    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        python -m pip install --upgrade pip
        pip install -r requirements.txt

    - name: Run build
      run: |
        echo "Building project..."
        # Add your build commands here

    - name: Send notification with PR details
      uses: dawidd6/action-slack-notify@v2
      env:
        SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        SLACK_MESSAGE: "PR Title: ${GITHUB_EVENT_NAME}, Description: ${GITHUB_EVENT.payload.pull_request.body}"

    - name: Commit and push changes with PR title
      run: |
        git config --global user.name 'GitHub Actions'
        git config --global user.email 'actions@github.com'
        git commit --allow-empty -m "Build triggered by PR: ${GITHUB_EVENT_NAME}"
        git push origin HEAD:${{ github.head_ref }}
